texto: Can I play a southern character? You betcha. Can I do the voice of Tourist Dad and Carnival Barker? Ya betcha. Can I do Fix-It Felix? Ya betcha. But I don't want to just play southern characters, so I hide it; I bury it. I tamp it down like a secret. Like a dirty little secret.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rob Huebel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Can I play a southern character? You betcha. Can I do the voice of Tourist Dad and Carnival Barke…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, character, want pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de want e play.

frase_original: Can I play a southern character? You betcha. Can I do the voice of Tourist Dad and Carnival Barker? Ya betcha. Can I do Fix-It Felix? Ya betcha. But I don't want to just play southern characters, so I hide it; I bury it. I tamp it down like a secret. Like a dirty little secret.
